recommendation: Reinforce two authoring lessons from this CR's own gate history: (1) a pairwise-consistency bar for discrimination, if written to compare Givens literally, over-fires on the corpus's own convention of a general scenario plus a narrower specialization sibling -- 'specialization is not contradiction' must be an explicit exception clause in any future pairwise bar, not left implicit (the same lesson independently reached in Op6-m2's own retro). (2) A frozen-scenario narrowing in flight with no ledger record let the conductor misjudge two identically-scoped When steps as different operations -- recommend a narrowing's ledger correction be written BEFORE it is acted on, not after.
evidence: 263-op6-m2-discrimination-bar.log.jsonl round 1: two frozen narrowings in flight with no ledger record; conductor wrongly cleared a contradicting pair by reading two identically-scoped When steps as different operations

same log round 2: spec-judge ALIGNED but flagged 'the pairwise rule as authored would over-fire on a convention the corpus relies on everywhere: a general scenario and a specific sibling whose narrower Given carves out an exception'
same log round 3: impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false -- the new checks carried no lens tag and the three-lens summary was not updated, violating the judge's own Output contract

recommendation: A context-line-reassignment edit silently orphaned an assertion off a frozen scenario onto the wrong one -- invisible to a raw line-diff, caught only by the cold spec-judge. Reinforces the existing structural-diff-over-raw-line-diff doctrine (cross-ref sdd-scenario-step-diff, this same batch): recommend the pre-gate mechanical sweep specifically flag any edit whose line-diff touches the boundary between two adjacent scenarios, since that is exactly the shape this bug takes.
evidence: d1-pre-judge-semantic-checks.log.jsonl: 'cold spec-judge caught a frozen-scenario narrowing an insert Edit introduced -- the every touched reference resolving scenario lost its And the gate spawns the cold judge assertion (orphaned onto the new last scenario by context-line reassignment; invisible as a raw line-diff)'
same log: a coverage-gap follow-on (stale error string after scope widened) closed same round, no new pattern

recommendation: Mutation testing caught a coverage gap a passing test suite hid: the format-flag scenario drove the render helpers directly but never exercised main()'s --format flag path, so a mutation hard-coding the CLI's format ignored the flag and every test stayed green. Reinforces the existing doctrine that a scenario's test must drive the entry point the Given/When actually names, not an internal helper one layer in -- recommend impl-judge mutation probes specifically target the CLI/entry-point binding for any scenario naming a flag or CLI behavior.
evidence: github-189-collision-ladder.log.jsonl: 'the test exercised renderVerdictToon + JSON.stringify directly but never drove main()'s --format flag path, so a mutation hard-coding the CLI format to json ... left all tests green'

recommendation: Three README passages kept describing the pre-CR 3-rung ladder after a 4th rung was added -- the frozen suite and the mermaid diagram had moved, the prose narrative had not. A routine architect-lens catch, but the third instance in this same combat-log batch of 'derived prose outliving the contract it describes' (cross-ref github-221 seq 'stale-comment-outlived-the-design-it-described', github-238's stale-claim finding). Recommend the Council treat this as a corpus-wide recurring pattern (see the separate pattern entry filed this retro), not three unrelated one-offs.
evidence: github-189-symbol-rung.log.jsonl: 'three README.md passages left describing the pre-CR 3-rung ladder ... contradicting the file's own updated mermaid/steps/Use-Cases-table and the frozen suite'

recommendation: Two real gaps in one mission: a builder coverage gap (no error-case scenario for an unresolvable artifact-type) and an impl defect mutation testing caught (artifactType was computed but never surfaced into the returned Correction, so two frozen scenarios were technically unmet while the producer's own tests still passed). Reinforces that a computed-but-unsurfaced value is a specific, recurring impl-gap shape distinct from a plain coverage-gap -- recommend impl-judge mutation probes specifically check that every value a scenario names is actually observable in the returned artifact, not just computed internally.
evidence: github-189-touch-set-correction.log.jsonl round 1: builder FAIL -- no error-case scenario for an unresolvable artifact-type; also a premature @frozen tag staged before the spec gate ('freeze is the gate's result, not producer-staged')

same log round 2: 'assembleCorrection computed each file's artifactType but never surfaced it into the returned Correction ... two frozen scenarios were unmet and the producer's tests passed even when the value was garbled'

recommendation: Builder coverage gaps (missing selection-branch and migrate-idempotency scenarios) closed routinely, but the impl gate's own adversarial mutation found a genuine non-blocking content gap: no test pinned the POST-MIGRATE coexistence state (orphan ref present AND the stale in-tree file still present, by design), so a precedence regression reverting every post-migrate repo to the stale file would have passed the full suite. Recommend generalizing this: any 'migrate but never delete the old artifact' design needs an explicit coexistence-state test, not just a happy-path migrate test.
evidence: github-190-autonomous-dispatch.log.jsonl: 'no test pinned the post-migrate COEXISTENCE state ... a resolveBackend precedence regression reverting every post-migrate repo to the stale in-tree file would pass the full suite'

recommendation: Clean Ship (17 scenario-overlap + 5 formation scenarios, zero rejects), but the impl-judge's own mutation probe surfaced a coverage gap on the writes-nothing frozen scenarios (a stray write would have survived the unit tests) and the conductor hardened it proactively before the judge had to reject. Recommend generalizing the pattern: a 'this component writes nothing' contract needs a static write-boundary guard (no fs write API imported) as a standard companion check, not just a behavioral assertion.
evidence: 191-corpus-partitioning-prereqs.log.jsonl: 'Mutation probe surfaced a test-coverage gap on the writes-nothing frozen scenarios (a stray write would survive the unit tests). Hardened proactively (judge did not reject): added a static write-boundary guard (engine imports no fs write API) + a behavioral guard'

recommendation: This mission is the strongest evidence yet for a rule already informally known but never ratified: do not specify a fold/consistency rule 'by example' (N independently-asserted scenarios with no stated closed-form rule). The barrier-fence block here went scenario-count 10->14->17 and contradiction-count 1->1->3 over 3 judge rounds -- each fix refuted by the next round, diverging rather than converging -- because Given-pair overlap grows quadratically with each added example and no fold rule was ever stated to check new scenarios against. The owner's cap-hit directive (split the mission, ship the two clean halves, re-file the fence RULE-FIRST as issue #224 with the derived closed-form R'' and its termination proof) is the correct doctrine; recommend codifying 'a consistency/fold rule must be stated in closed form before scenarios are added, and iteration-cap-hit is presented to the owner rather than auto-accepted' as a standing spec-producer rule for any node computing a fold over a graph or set.
evidence: 192-barriers-and-blast.log.jsonl: scenario-count 10->14->17, contradiction-count 1->1->3 across 3 judge-iteration rounds on the barrier-fence block; iteration cap reached without convergence

same log, owner directive: 'Root cause diagnosed as a METHOD error: the fence was specified by example ... each added scenario created new chances to contradict ... re-file it as issue #224 to be designed RULE-FIRST from R'' + its termination proof'

recommendation: Five of seven real defects in this mission's impl gate were invisible to the frozen suite because every fixture used a CONSTRUCTED corpus (the suite's own preamble mandates this for determinism), while the defects only manifested against the real repo: count-vs-coverage (every fixture built exactly the count threshold), reinvented node-recovery (every test's two roots collapsed to one), relative-path corruption (every fixture used an absolute tmpdir root), toml-parser strictness (fixture picked the one byte-shape that parses), and mention-based centrality (fixtures never exercised the corpus's real reference forms). Recommend making a live-corpus smoke check (asserting properties, not magic numbers, in a file outside the frozen suite) a STANDARD requirement for any node that computes a metric over the live repo -- this mission's own smoke check is now empirically proven necessary (it fails 3/6 when the matcher is reverted, while the frozen suite still passes 30/30) and should be the reference precedent corpus-wide, not a one-off hardening.
evidence: 192-barriers-and-blast.log.jsonl: 'ROOT CAUSE across all five defects of this mission ... the frozen suite's own preamble mandates CONSTRUCTED corpora and never the live corpus, so the suite is structurally blind to the only property that determines whether this node has value'
same log: live-corpus smoke check's necessity 'EMPIRICALLY PROVEN, not argued: with the matcher mutated back to the pre-fix behavior, the smoke check FAILS 3 of 6 while the frozen suite passes 30/30'

same log: separate fail-loud discipline finding -- a sensitive-paths reader swallowed non-ENOENT read errors as benign 'no markings', silently UNDER-calling blast exactly where a project asked for care; 'a check that cannot classify must escalate, never default to the safe-looking answer'

recommendation: Two judge-iteration corrections, both closed additively without any re-open: a coverage-gap where prose asserted a use case ('briefs do not anchor presence') with no covering scenario, and a spec-feature-contradiction where added Then steps restated the gate's rationale instead of asserting a filesystem-observable fact. Routine instances of the two most common existing recurring causes (cross-ref this retro's pattern entry) -- no new lesson, logged for the recurrence count.
evidence: github-193-transient-cr-artifacts.log.jsonl: coverage-gap -- 'the briefs-do-not-anchor-presence use case was asserted in spec prose with no covering scenario ... added the staged-brief-absent-plan no-op scenario'
same log: spec-feature-contradiction -- 'two added Then steps restated the gate's rationale instead of a filesystem-observable fact'

recommendation: Notable self-referential finding: round 2's cold spec-judge (fresh context) refuted a CONDUCTOR decision, not a producer slip -- the plan had argued 'no spec-producer.feature change needed, the producer already loads suite-format' but the judge showed the producer's only self-check runs a purely mechanical form check, not the new duty's actual criterion, an unverified-coverage-claim that is exactly the defect class this CR itself specifies against. Recommend treating 'the conductor's own scoping decision, not just the producer's output' as squarely in-bounds for the spec-judge to refute -- this mission is a clean precedent that the judge caught the doctrine failing to apply itself, and it should be cited when training future spec-judge dispatches.
evidence: github-220-given-is-a-test-vector.log.jsonl round 2: 'It refuted a conductor decision, not a producer slip ... an unverified coverage claim is precisely the defect class this CR exists to catch, so the CR was held to its own standard'

same log, later hardening: the impl-judge's own non-blocking architect observation (an unrouted escalation could ride through as STATUS:complete) was escalated to the Council and fixed before the gate rather than deferred, explicitly because 'shipping an unrouted escalation would reproduce the CR's complaint inside its own fix'

recommendation: Densest single-mission evidence yet for two method findings worth ratifying corpus-wide: (1) 'a measured ceiling is not evidence a dimension works -- test loseability by ABLATING the rule and re-scoring, never by watching the mean against a correct subject' (a pre-registered demotion trigger could never fire because the observed floor was mistaken for a pass threshold). (2) 'verify mutant PENETRATION before scoring a mutation-test result -- a mutation that does not change behavior measures nothing', discovered when 3 of 4 conductor-supplied mutants failed to induce the degraded behavior they were meant to. Also recurring in this same mission (3rd instance in one combat log): derived prose (a stale comment, a README's dimension count) outliving the contract it describes.
evidence: github-221-binding-rubric-thresholds.log.jsonl 'pre-registered-trigger-was-unfalsifiable': 'cohesion-preserved was UNLOSEABLE ... A measured ceiling is not evidence a dimension works; it is equally consistent with a dimension that cannot fail'

same log 'METHOD FINDING -- penetration is the hidden variable in mutation-testing a rubric': '3 of 4 conductor-supplied mutants failed to induce the degraded BEHAVIOR they were meant to ... verify mutant PENETRATION before scoring'
same log: 3 separate stale-derived-prose corrections in one mission (SKILL.md grading claim, README barrier numbers, a stale scenario comment) -- same defect class recurring inside its own fix

recommendation: Two prose-impl-contradiction corrections stand out beyond the routine coverage-gap pair: (1) the ledger gained a 7th kind but the write-ownership matrix still enumerated six -- the exact governance a producer loads first to check whether an act is permitted silently said no to the doctrine's own unconditional act, and the headless agent's write-allowlist inherited the same omission in exactly the unattended case the design exists for. (2) A 'consumer sweep' report claimed every enumeration was reconciled while three actually remained -- 'a sweep that reports done while incomplete is the exact defect this CR specifies against'. Recommend a mechanical grep-count check (N sites claiming reconciliation vs N sites actually matching) before a sweep-completion report is trusted, since prose self-report already failed twice in one mission.
evidence: github-237-handoff-followups.log.jsonl: 'the ledger gained a 7th kind but the canonical write-ownership matrix still enumerated six ... the headless agent's write allowlist omitted it too, in exactly the unattended case the design exists for'

same log: 'the consumer sweep claimed every enumeration was reconciled while three remained ... a sweep that reports done while incomplete is the exact defect this CR specifies against, so the claim was made true rather than narrowed'

recommendation: Clean precedent for a specific move worth naming: when a frozen scenario is proven self-contradictory (here, two scenarios binding the same 1-area input and demanding opposite Thens), the fix that actually specifies the resolution is the ADDITIVE scenario stating the missing precondition -- not just narrowing the contradicting one, which 'leaves the size-1 answer unstated' since a decision argued only in an engine header/comment is not specified. Also notable: round 1's impl-judge caught the CR's own README transcribing the ORIGINAL ISSUE's over-claim without independently verifying it against the shipped engine -- 'the conductor TRANSCRIBED THE BRIEF INSTEAD OF CHECKING IT', the same failure mode the CR existed to fix, now found inside its own fix.
evidence: 238-blast-one-area-contract.log.jsonl: 'the added scenario is the load-bearing edit ... A decision argued in an engine header but not frozen in a scenario is NOT specified'

same log round 1: 'the ISSUE ITSELF carries the same over-claim, and the conductor TRANSCRIBED THE BRIEF INSTEAD OF CHECKING IT -- a correlated blind spot inherited from the source, exactly the failure mode the CR exists to fix in the suite'

same log: the guard the new scenario pins was previously 'COMPLETELY UNPROTECTED: nothing in the suite or the smoke check would have caught its deletion' -- a second live instance this retro of the constructed-fixture blind spot (cross-ref github-192, same batch)

recommendation: Two-layer defect worth generalizing beyond this mission: a builder coverage gap (every scenario asserted the guard fails closed; none asserted it stays quiet on input that parses -- 'an implementation hardcoded to always fail closed would satisfy the whole suite'), AND the FIX for that gap was itself unloseable on first attempt (the new test's fixture was both unparseable and hedged, so it passed without the parse verdict ever being exercised) -- caught only by re-running the ablation against the new test rather than trusting that adding a test closed the gap. Recommend: after adding a test meant to close a discovered gap, re-run the same ablation against the NEW test before considering the gap closed -- 'adding a test' and 'closing a gap' are not the same claim.
evidence: 263-op6-m1-suite-parse-guard.log.jsonl seq1: 'Every drafted scenario asserted the guard fails closed; none asserted it raises no violation on input that parses ... the safety dual of the fail-open defect the CR exists to close'
same log seq4 (self-review): 'The first tree-wide sweep test was itself unloseable ... Caught by re-running the ablation against the new test rather than trusting that adding a test closed the gap'

recommendation: Four consecutive spec-judge rounds on this one mission, each closing one instance of the SAME defect shape one sentence/row over from the last: an identity-exclusion matrix swept on one axis (DocString) while the sibling axis (DataTable) stayed half-done, so no scenario could register a miss on the unswept form while the prose still claimed universal coverage. Recommend the spec-judge's builder lens explicitly check any 'exclusion matrix' or 'per-form property' claim by requiring one miss-registering scenario PER cell/row, not per claim -- a whole-matrix claim closed cell-by-cell across 4 rounds is exactly the shape a single mechanical completeness check would have caught in round 1.
evidence: github-278-hash-step-arguments.log.jsonl round 3: 'the identity matrix was swept on the DocString row across both faces but left the DataTable row half-done ... the sweep itself had the named-site shape the prior rounds diagnosed'

same log round 4: 'the prose universally claimed every form exclusion is an independent implementation choice, but the indentation row is a parser property with no field to exclude and no mutant caught by it alone' -- a 4th recurrence of the same named-site defect shape in one mission

recommendation: Clean Ship, single-round, no corrections -- the mission's own handoff filed 3 backlog followups rather than treating the observation gaps as blocking, a reasonable and low-drama precedent. Nothing to recommend beyond noting the clean pass; logged to satisfy the distillation requirement.
evidence: github-349-governance-provenance-check.log.jsonl: single report line, outcome=pass, 'implemented governances_loaded declaration ... run inline by the conductor rather than a spawned builder -- a detail-adjustment given the doc-only, low-blast change'
corresponding ledger gate lines: spec approve + impl approve, both by:agent, zero correction lines

recommendation: Clean Ship. The one notable event was a squad re-resolution (ACED recuses on SDD's own process-Gherkin, re-routes to SDD defaults) -- an established, already-precedented pattern (same path as github-191, github-220), not a new finding. Logged to satisfy the distillation requirement; no new recommendation.
evidence: handoff-pr-closes-ref.log.jsonl: 'ACED squad ... recuses on the handoff subject: handoff.feature is SDD process-behavior Gherkin ... not an agent-config eval suite ... Re-resolved this unit chain to SDD defaults. Not a halt.'

recommendation: This mission shipped the `distills` mechanism this very Scanner run now depends on -- and its own impl-gate correction flagged a real enum-precision gap worth ratifying: a correction whose actual shape was 'the plan-retirement operating docs contradicted the shipped mechanical gate' (a prose-impl-contradiction by the existing enum's own definition) was tagged cause:spec-feature-contradiction in the combat log, with the conductor itself noting 'Nearest-bucket cause: this is an operating-doc-vs-shipped-impl contradiction, not spec.md-vs-.feature -- flagged as a candidate enum-precision follow-up.' Recommend the Council confirm producer/conductor guidance sharpens the spec-feature-contradiction vs prose-impl-contradiction boundary (spec.md-vs-.feature is the former; a skill's own operating docs vs its shipped code is the latter, already in the enum) so future corrections self-tag correctly -- this Scanner's own recurring-pattern tally (this retro's separate pattern entry) had to read past this exact mistagging to count causes correctly.
evidence: plan-retirement-distill-gate.log.jsonl impl-gate correction: cause recorded as 'spec-feature-contradiction' but detail text reads 'Nearest-bucket cause: this is an operating-doc-vs-shipped-impl contradiction, not spec.md-vs-.feature -- flagged as a candidate enum-precision follow-up'

combat-log-governance already defines prose-impl-contradiction as 'a skill's own operating docs or a sibling design doc asserted behavior the shipped implementation no longer has' -- matching this correction's actual shape

recommendation: Clean Ship, single correction-free report: a rebase conflict was resolved confidently as orthogonal deliver work (target reworded one paragraph, this CR inserted an adjacent one) because the frozen scenarios were unchanged by the merge. A reasonable precedent for when a rebase conflict may be self-resolved vs escalated -- worth citing alongside the sibling rebase-conflict-refinements CR (not in this batch) as the confident-resolution reference pair.
evidence: rebase-before-impl-gate.log.jsonl: 'Resolved confidently as deliver code work (orthogonal concerns) ... the frozen rebase scenarios are unchanged by the merge, so confidence was high; not a halt. Dogfoods this CR's own rebase-before-gate + confident-resolution doctrine.'

recommendation: A design-overreach correction worth generalizing: the first-drafted fix invented a NEW fail-closed outcome for a still-frozen narrowing, when the correct fix was routing the existing narrowing-detection to the EXISTING Clearance floor rather than adding a parallel mechanism. Recommend a standing spec-producer check: before authoring a new mechanism for 'edit X should be blocked/escalated', check whether an existing floor (Clearance, in this case) already owns that duty -- a coverage gap should usually route to an existing gate, not spawn a new one.
evidence: sdd-scenario-step-diff.log.jsonl: 'design-overreach ... The authored pre-filter invented a fail-closed outcome for a still-frozen narrowing, which contradicts the standing narrowing->Clearance model ... Reframing: the fix is that the edit-class CLASSIFICATION must be a per-scenario structural diff ... so a narrowing is correctly detected and routed to the EXISTING Clearance floor'

recommendation: A testability-defect builder-lens rejection (non-observable Then clause + coverage gap + prose-only reconciliation) closed in one round -- routine. Notable context: this mission ran on the 'portable cold-subagent fallback' because the warm-dispatch wiring it specs is not yet self-hosting; worth the Council tracking whether that fallback path gets its own coverage once warm-dispatch ships, since a fallback path that only ever gets exercised by missions specifying the thing it falls back FROM has thin real-world coverage.
evidence: wire-cyberlegion-dispatch.log.jsonl: 'spec-gate round1 builder-lens FAIL (non-observable Then clause + coverage gap + prose-only reconciliation); fixed by narrowing scenario, adding self-contained bridge + prefer-warm-judge scenarios; round2 ALIGNED'
same log, handoff: 'mission ran on the portable cold-subagent fallback (the warm-dispatch wiring it specs is not yet self-hosting), so no warm units to reset'

recommendation: Reinforce the already-flagged (unratified) pre-judge coverage-gap check (cross-ref strategy.ba6a39 seq1, strategy.317dd8 seq1): this retro's fresh batch of previously-undistilled Ship missions adds 6 new coverage-gap and 5 new spec-feature-contradiction judge-iteration instances, all closed same-session by a cold judge that a mechanical pre-judge check could have caught earlier. Recurrence now spans well over 10 independent missions across 3+ retro batches. Separately, one of this batch's own corrections (plan-retirement-distill-gate, see its own distillation entry) was itself mistagged spec-feature-contradiction when prose-impl-contradiction was the better existing fit -- recommend the Council also ratify sharper producer/conductor guidance on that enum boundary alongside the coverage-gap check.
evidence: coverage-gap judge-iteration corrections this retro: d1-pre-judge-semantic-checks, github-193-transient-cr-artifacts, github-263-op6-m1 (x2), github-237-handoff-followups, plan-retirement-distill-gate -- 6 instances

spec-feature-contradiction judge-iteration corrections this retro: d1-pre-judge-semantic-checks, github-193-transient-cr-artifacts, github-237-handoff-followups (x2), plan-retirement-distill-gate -- 5 instances (one of the five self-flagged as mistagged, see plan-retirement-distill-gate's own distillation entry)
cross-ref: strategy.ba6a39 seq1 (already unratified, prior retro) and strategy.317dd8 seq1 first flagged this exact recurrence
